transport_readonly: vsp transport list/get are read-only but gated off by default (error: 'transports not enabled (use --enable-transports or SAP_ENABLE_TRANSPORTS=true)'). With SAP_ENABLE_TRANSPORTS=true: 'transport list' -> columns NUMBER/OWNER/STATUS/TYPE/DESCRIPTION, 6 transport(s), exit 0. 'transport get S4HK903951' -> header (Owner HUB2150, Type K, Status Modifiable, Target S4V, Desc '[TRN] JNC ABAP TEST 2026.07.06') + Tasks(2: S4HK903953/S4HK903952) + Objects(1: R3TR PROG ZTEST01), exit 0. No write attempted. Note: --transport-read-only is an MCP-server flag and is rejected as a CLI flag ('unknown flag').
atc_error_exit: Genuine attempt to induce an Error-grade ATC finding via PROG ZSAH4_ATCPROBE (hardcoded password literal 'Init1234' + dynamic-WHERE SQL-injection pattern: SELECT ... WHERE (lv_where) with lv_where built from a PARAMETERS input). IDES default variant (no --variant) produced NO Error-grade finding -- only 2 INFO (SLIN: line 1 time-zone config; line 10 untranslated 'Init1234'), Total 2 finding(s), exit 0. The hardcoded password and dynamic SQL were NOT flagged as security findings (default variant lacks those checks). No forcing per task. Conclusion: whether an Error-grade ATC finding elevates vsp exit>0 remains UNVERIFIED on this system's default variant (consistent with COMMANDS.md sec 4 / VERIFY-PATTERNS sec 2-4 long-standing open item).
